topic: Automation-related security bugs in HackerOne reports
query_groups: rate limit bypass brute force authentication
OTP verification code MFA brute force
SMS invite email notification spam mass mailing
GraphQL batching cost limit negative rate
race condition parallel concurrent double submit
replay signature reuse idempotency payment token
webhook SSRF DoS notification spam automation
XMLRPC multicall brute force DoS bulk wordpress
business logic abuse coupon credit reward vote follow bulk
2FA bypass brute force verification code Slack
invite code brute force Uber promo code enumeration
email flooding password reset mass email no rate limit
hackbot redacted data brute force duplication

retrieval_precision.justification: Most retrieved results were directly relevant to automation abuse; some noise from generic SSRF/rate-limit reports that are not automation-specific.

retrieval_recall.justification: Coverage is strong for rate-limit, captcha, webhook, and brute-force families. Weaker for race conditions, replay, and business-logic automation which required more targeted queries.

source_grounding.justification: Every cited report was verified from KB documents with matching filenames, titles, programs, and evidence excerpts. No unverified claims.

classification_quality.justification: Clear 5-category taxonomy (A-E) applied consistently. Some borderline cases between B (missing control) and A (user abuse) required judgment calls.

deduplication_quality.justification: XMLRPC cluster (325040, 448524, 752073, 884756) properly grouped as near-duplicates. Some remaining duplication in email-flooding and captcha-bypass families could be tighter.

safety.justification: All guidance is defensive and authorized. No live exploit instructions against third-party systems. Testing methodology focuses on controlled verification.

artifact_usefulness.justification: Four well-structured artifacts covering patterns, eval queries, verified reports, and summary. JSONL dataset enables automated retrieval testing. Pattern taxonomy is actionable for engineering teams.

overall_score.justification: Strong coverage of automation-related security bugs with verified source grounding. Main gap is in race-condition and replay families which are less represented in the KB. Deduplication could be improved for repetitive report clusters.

weakest_or_noisy_areas: Race condition reports are present but few are automation-specific
Replay/signature reuse has only 2 strong reports
GraphQL batching abuse has only 2 reports (481518 is excellent but isolated)
XMLRPC cluster is repetitive - 4 near-identical reports
Business logic automation (SDEX) is strong but isolated
Some SSRF webhook results were not automation-specific (just standard SSRF)
recommended_kb_improvements: Add a summary doc for race-condition patterns in HackerOne reports
Add a summary doc for replay/idempotency failures
Create a deduplication index for XMLRPC and captcha-bypass report clusters
Index more GraphQL batching and cost-limit bypass reports
Add automation-specific tags or metadata to report files for filtering
